headline: Walmart Announces 2026 Annual Shareholders’ Meeting Voting Results
summary: BENTONVILLE, Ark., June 04, 2026--Walmart Inc. (Nasdaq: WMT) today announced preliminary shareholder voting results for its Annual Shareholders’ Meeting. Approximately 89.88% of all outstanding shares were represented at the meeting.

headline: Walmart warns higher prices may be on the way
summary: Walmart has long been a go-to destination for consumers looking to stretch their budgets during periods of economic uncertainty. For decades, shoppers have turned to Walmart for affordable groceries, household goods, and basic essentials when they needed to make every dollar count. That role has ...

headline: Walmart Adds Express Delivery From In-Store Restaurants, Starting With Subway®
summary: BENTONVILLE, Ark., June 04, 2026--Walmart is making it even easier for customers to get everything they need in one seamless shopping experience, including freshly made meals from Subway. Beginning this month, customers in select locations can order Subway directly through the Walmart app or Walmart.com and have their meal delivered in as little as 30 minutes or less, either on its own or alongside their Walmart Express Delivery order.

headline: Why Walmart and Other Consumer Stocks Aren’t Getting the Gains They Deserve
summary: Yardeni Research Contributing Editor Jackie Doherty highlights several companies she thinks delivered strong results in their latest quarters:  Dollar General  attracted more six-figure households and boosted its earnings-per-share target, while  Ulta Beauty  delivered broad-based growth.  RBC Capital Markets calls out Walmart and Ollie’s Bargain Outlet Holdings “as among the best positioned to navigate the current environment.”  Looking at the sector overall, both the  exchange-traded fund and the State Street Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR ETF are lower since the start of the year, even as the  is up double digits in percent terms.

headline: Walmart Tests Loyalty And Premium Food With Canada Membership And Smokehouse Beef
summary: Walmart (NasdaqGS:WMT) has launched its Walmart+ membership program in Canada, its first international rollout of the service. The Canadian Walmart+ offering combines delivery, free shipping, and entertainment benefits for subscribers. In Texas, Walmart introduced an exclusive McClaren Farms Smokehouse Angus beef line, focused on premium, specialty meat products. For investors watching Walmart, these moves show the retailer extending its membership model beyond the U.S. and testing how...

headline: Walmart CEO John Furner worked his way up from the garden center. After 30 years, he’s sharing the one trait that matters most in his job
summary: After rising from hourly associate to CEO, Walmart’s John Furner says embracing change was key—a view shared by execs at Macy’s, Xerox, and Informatica.

headline: Five Below's Beat-And-Raise Quarter May End Its Growth Trajectory
summary: Investors were spooked by the prospect that Five Below's growth rate might have reached its peak.  Five Below stock sank 10% at Thursday's open, according to MarketSurge.  Meanwhile, adjusted earnings per share were $2.22, 25% above the $1.77 looked for by Wall Street.

headline: Walmart+ arrives in Canada, with unlimited delivery, free shipping with no order minimum and Crave, for only $8.97/month - the cost of a single delivery
summary: Walmart+ has arrived in Canada, unlocking a first-of-its-kind membership program that goes beyond delivery for Canadians, bringing together unlimited same-day delivery from store, free shipping with no order minimum from Walmart.ca and a subscription to Crave – all for $8.97 per month or $89 annually.

headline: Walmart Tool Push Tests Home Depot Thesis On DIY Customers And Value
summary: Walmart has launched an exclusive Greenworks Pro professional power tools line across its stores and website. The move brings pro-grade cordless tools to Walmart shoppers and targets customers doing smaller repairs and everyday home projects. This expansion in hardware offerings increases competitive pressure on Home Depot (NYSE:HD) in the DIY-focused tools category. Home Depot, traded as NYSE:HD, has long focused on home improvement products and services for both professionals and DIY...
